title: Ben Hellewell
extract:
Ben Hellewell (born 30 January 1992) is a former Scotland international rugby league footballer who last played as a second-row forward for Salford Red Devils in the Super League.
He was contracted to the Warrington Wolves in the Super League, playing on loan from Warrington at the York City Knights in the Championship and the Barrow Raiders in Championship 1. He played for Featherstone Rovers in the Co-operative Championship, and on loan from Featherstone at the Dewsbury Rams in the Championship. He has also played for the London Broncos in the Championship and in the Super League, and on loan from the Broncos at the Sheffield Eagles in the Championship. Hellewell also played for the Leigh Centurions in the second-tier and the Super League. Earlier in his career he played as a centre, fullback and winger.
In 2019 he helped the Eagles to win the inaugural 1895 Cup as they defeated the Widnes Vikings 36–18 in the final. He has earned 9 caps for Scotland in the European competitions and the 2013 Rugby League World Cup.
